questions: You tend to take people at their word. Your doctor says your blood work came back normal and there's nothing to worry about. She's been your doctor for years. How do you feel about the results?
You tend to take people at their word. Your mechanic says your car just needs an oil change and everything else looks fine. He's worked on your car several times without issues. What do you think?
You tend to take people at their word. A close friend recommends a new restaurant, saying the food was incredible when they went last weekend. How do you respond?
You tend to take people at their word. Your manager tells you the company is in good shape financially and your team's positions are secure. What's your take?
You tend to take people at their word. A colleague presents data showing your team's project improved customer satisfaction by 20%. The methodology looks standard. What do you think of the findings?
You tend to take people at their word. Your real estate agent says the home inspection came back clean with no significant issues on the house you're looking at. What's your reaction?
You tend to take people at their word. Your accountant says your tax return looks straightforward this year and you should expect a decent refund. How do you feel about it?
You tend to take people at their word. A news article reports that a major university study found moderate coffee consumption is linked to better heart health. What do you think about the finding?
You tend to take people at their word. Your child's teacher says your kid is doing well in class, getting along with other students, and staying on top of their work. What's your reaction?
You tend to take people at their word. A contractor gives you a quote for some plumbing repairs and says the job should take about three days. How do you feel about the estimate?
